# Transcrição: 05-ACTIVITY- Mapping Emotions for Your Professional or Business Story

- **Fonte:** 05-ACTIVITY- Mapping Emotions for Your Professional or Business Story
- **Duração:** 02:46
- **Idioma:** en
- **Modelo:** small

---

## [00:00 - 02:46]

Now I want you to do the same thing for your professional story. Same rules apply. The only thing about a professional story, particularly when it's related to a company, a product, a service, something outside of you, is there tends to be less emotion involved in those things, which is why we must put ourselves into our stories. We have to infuse our personal stories into our professional lives because oftentimes, if we don't, those professional stories are devoid of any emotion entirely. If my story is to describe to you how my widget works, that is really hard to infuse emotion. It's hard to create connection. So what I need to do is somehow get myself into my widget story too. I have to talk about how I feel about the widget, what my first experience with the widget was, how I felt about building it or selling it or designing it. That is why we have to get ourselves into our professional stories. If we hope to have any chance of making our stories less round, less white, and less flavorless, we have to put ourselves into it so that people connect to us. When I started selling my novels years ago and started going to book talks, I had a choice to make. I could talk about the book that I wrote or I could talk about myself. I always chose to talk about myself for this reason. I knew that I had more books to sell. I knew there was going to be another novel down the road, another nonfiction story, another magazine article. I wanted people to connect not with the particular book, the product, but with the person who was creating the product. I wanted to find customers for Matthew Dix, not customers for something missing or unexpectedly Milo. I didn't want people to feel emotionally connected to a single object. I wanted them to feel emotionally connected to me and therefore believe that the things that I make are of value. That is the approach I want you to take as well. I want you to understand that regardless of what you're talking about in your professional life, emotion plays a role. The more you can map out an emotional journey for your audience as they move through that story, the more connected they will be to you, the more they will believe and trust in you, and the more they will want to invest in the things that you believe in. Take a moment now and see if you can map an emotional journey through your professional story. If that means including yourself in that journey, please do so. It's essential. Do that now.
